prospect.name: Maya Krishnan
prospect.title: VP Sales
prospect.headline: VP Sales at Ferrovia | Building the bridge between what sales leaders plan and what reps actually execute
prospect.about: I've spent the last decade watching good sales strategies die in the last mile — between what the leadership team decides and what lands in a rep's Monday morning. At Ferrovia I'm trying to rebuild our go-to-market around execution, not just forecasting. Always happy to compare notes with other sales leaders wrestling with the same thing.

experience.title: VP Sales
experience.description: Leading GTM for a 34-person sales team across NAM and EMEA. Rebuilding playbook infrastructure and deal execution standards.

experience.title: Sr. Director of Sales
experience.description: Scaled enterprise sales from $8M to $34M ARR. Built out mid-market motion from zero to $9M.

account.name: Ferrovia Analytics
account.one_line_description: Supply chain visibility platform for mid-market manufacturers and logistics operators.

account_events.description: New CRO hired 6 weeks ago (Devin Osei, previously VP Sales at Formica Data).
Series B extension raised ($22M, led by Emergence Capital).
18 open AE and SDR requisitions posted in last 60 days.

posts.text: Three months into building out our sales playbook and the thing nobody warns you about: writing the playbook is 10% of the work. Getting reps to actually run it is the other 90%. Our forecast accuracy is still swinging by 20+ points quarter to quarter because what's in Salesforce isn't what's happening in the field. Curious how other sales leaders are closing that gap — genuinely asking, not pitching anything.

Hot take: the reason most sales playbooks fail isn't because the playbook is wrong. It's because nobody can tell you on a Tuesday afternoon whether a deal is following it or not. We're drowning in process documentation and starving for execution visibility.

comments.text: This resonates. We went through exactly this last year. The missing piece for us was real-time signal on whether reps were actually doing the things we said they should — not just tracking outcomes after the fact.
comments.parent_post_summary: A post by a RevOps leader arguing that most sales metrics are lagging indicators and you need leading indicators to course-correct.

reactions.parent_post_summary: A post about the cost of CRM data hygiene and why RevOps teams burn out trying to fix it.

product.name: GoWarmCRM
product.one_liner: A sales execution platform that sits on top of your CRM and operationalizes your playbooks — so you can see whether reps are actually running them, not just whether deals are closing.

value_props: Closes the execution gap between defined sales process and what reps actually do.
Surfaces deal-level actions from your playbook, not just stage transitions.
Works with your existing CRM — Salesforce, HubSpot, Pipedrive — rather than replacing it.
Gives sales leaders leading indicators on execution quality, not just lagging indicators on pipeline.
target_personas: VP Sales at growth-stage B2B SaaS
CROs inheriting a sales team and trying to diagnose execution gaps
